title: Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council considers allowing non-citizens with legal status to vote in local elections.
Councilor Julia Mejia's petition revives a similar stalled 2023 effort for non-citizen voting rights.
More than 28% of Boston's population are non-citizens, contributing $2.3 billion annually in taxes.

title: ‘He looks great’: Draymond Green had high praise for Jayson Tatum
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Draymond Green praised Jayson Tatum's performance, noting his impressive return with 24 points and 10 rebounds.
Green, Tatum's former Olympic teammate, commented on Tatum's ability to move well and handle the ball post-injury.
Despite playful trash-talk, Green expressed happiness in seeing Tatum back on the court during Celtics' 120-99 win over Warriors.

title: Two-time cancer survivor from Easton to run Boston Marathon for Mass General
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kevin O'Rourke from Easton will run the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting Massachusetts General Hospital.
O'Rourke is honoring his late father, Brian, who received cancer treatment at Mass General, extending his life by 26 years.
A two-time cancer survivor, O'Rourke is running his first marathon since treatment, inspired by personal and family experiences.

title: Boston police officer arrested, charged with manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston police officer Nicholas O'Malley arrested, charged with manslaughter in Stephenson King's March 11 shooting death.
Incident occurred during response to carjacking in Roxbury; King allegedly accelerated vehicle into a police cruiser.
Two Boston city councilors urge release of body camera footage for transparency; investigation by Suffolk DA ongoing.

title: Police fatally shoot person with knife at Weymouth park, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person armed with a knife was fatally shot by police at Webb Memorial State Park in Weymouth.
The incident occurred around 4 p.m. after police responded to a 911 call reporting a person in distress.
The shooting is being investigated by Massachusetts State Police and the Norfolk District Attorney’s Office.

title: A botanical garden in New England just ranked among the best in America
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Coastal Maine Botanical Gardens ranked No. 7 among top American botanical gardens by USA Today readers.
Located in Boothbay, Maine, it features 325 acres of gardens, woods, coastal waterfront, and themed gardens.
The gardens open May 1, with tickets priced at $28 for adults, and offer activities like a sculpture trail.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 19, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Bambola and The Girl Next Door open in Seaport, offering Southern Italian dishes and street food.
Common Craft debuts in South Boston with a seasonal menu led by chef Tony Messina.
Roger’s Fish Co. launches at Logan Airport's Terminal A, serving New England seafood classics.

title: Watch: Stephen Curry wasn’t thrilled about a fan grabbing his arm at TD Garden
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A fan grabbed Stephen Curry's arm at TD Garden, leading to their removal by security.
Curry missed the game due to a knee injury as the Warriors lost to the Celtics 120-99.
Warriors coach Steve Kerr commented on the team's struggle without Curry, who is recovering from injury.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum show what’s possible in Celtics’ win over Warriors: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 32 points and Jayson Tatum added 24 to lead the Celtics to a 120-99 win over the Warriors.
Former Celtic Kristaps Porzingis received a warm welcome upon returning to TD Garden, playing five games with the Warriors.
Robert Parish, promoting his memoir, praised the current Celtics but claimed his 1986 Celtics would win due to Bill Walton.

title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises the Boston Celtics' rise from an 'afterthought' to title contenders without Jayson Tatum.
The Celtics swept the Warriors in the regular season and are currently second in the Eastern Conference.
Boston's increased depth came from utilizing younger players during Tatum's absence, enhancing their team dynamics.
